items.title: Apple quietly launches AirPods Max 2
items.content: The successor to its premium headphones cost $549 and are launching with enhanced active noise cancellation, the H2 chip, live translation, better sound quality, and more.

items.title: Netflix’s ‘Frankenstein’ wins three Oscars, ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ wins two
items.content: “Frankenstein” won for Best Production Design, Best Costume Design, and Best Makeup and Hairstyling, while “KPop Demon Hunters” won for Best Animated Feature and Best Original

items.title: The MacBook Neo is ‘the most repairable MacBook’ in years, according to iFixit
items.content: Apple’s new MacBook Neo isn’t just the most affordable MacBook — it’s also the company's most repairable laptop in “about fourteen years."

items.title: Apple drops commission rates in China without a fuss
items.content: Apple dropped its App Store commission rates to 25% in China, and commissions on auto-renewed subscriptions down to 12%.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
items.content: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: The public opposition to AI infrastructure is heating up
items.content: Public backlash over the data center boom is leading to a variety of draconian policies — including bans on new construction.

items.title: All the important news from the ongoing India AI Impact Summit
items.content: India is hosting a four-day AI Summit this week that will be attended by executives from major AI labs and Big Tech, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, an

items.title: With Iran War, Trump Risks Stepping on Gains From His Own Tax Cuts
items.content: President Trump’s war in Iran has raised some costs just as many Americans are starting to see savings from last year’s tax cuts.

items.title: Why Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Problem Is Going Global
items.content: President Trump is getting little to no support so far for his call that other countries do their part to try to ease an energy crisis prompted by U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran

items.title: Iran War Risks Sharpening Fed Divisions Over Rate Cuts
items.content: The Federal Reserve is set to hold interest rates steady this week as officials grapple with a host of new economic risks stemming from the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Rising Prices and High Interest Rates Are Making Car Ownership Feel Impossible
items.content: Rising vehicle prices, auto loan interest rates, and insurance and maintenance costs are making it harder for people to buy or keep cars.
